notes: Verification-only task: all 171 tests (150 unit + 2 export integration + 19 M5 type integration) pass. All Value variants (Enum, List, Set, Map, Option), Type variants (Enum, List, Set, Map, Option, Function), trait definition types, Ord, Display, content_hash implementations confirmed present and working. No code changes needed.

summary: Add lambda_expression and lambda_param rules to tree-sitter-reify/grammar.js, then regenerate the parser with `tree-sitter generate`. Lambda syntax: `|params| body` where params are `identifier` or `identifier : type_expr`, comma-separated. Add lambda_expression to _expression choice (not _primary_expression) to give it proper precedence. Must handle `|` vs `||` — add to conflicts if needed.; Add ExprKind::Lambda { params: Vec<LambdaParam>, body: Box<Expr> } and LambdaParam { name: String, type_expr: Option<TypeExpr>, span: SourceSpan } to reify-syntax/src/lib.rs AST types.; Add CompiledExprKind::Lambda { params: Vec<(String, Option<Type>)>, body: Box<CompiledExpr>, captures: Vec<ValueCellId> } to reify-types/src/expr.rs. Add a CompiledExpr::lambda() constructor with content_hash tag byte [7] (using a distinct tag). Also add Value::Lambda { params: Vec<String>, body: Box<CompiledExpr>, captures: ValueMap } to reify-types/src/value.rs with content_hash tag byte [12], Display, PartialEq, Eq, Ord implementations.; Update reify-types/src/lib.rs to export the new types (if LambdaParam etc are placed there). Ensure CompiledExprKind::Lambda and Value::Lambda are visible to downstream crates.

Write failing parser test: parse `|x| x * 2` as a lambda_expression node in tree-sitter, verify no parse errors. Add test in crates/reify-syntax/tests/lambda_tests.rs.; Add lower_lambda_expression() to ts_parser.rs: handle 'lambda_expression' node kind by extracting lambda_param children (name + optional type_expr) and body expression. Wire it into lower_expr() dispatch. This should make step-1 pass.; Write failing parser test: parse `|x: Real, y: Real| x + y` → Lambda with 2 typed params. Verify param names and type annotations are correctly extracted.; Ensure typed lambda params are correctly lowered — the optional `: type_expr` part in lambda_param should produce LambdaParam with type_expr: Some(TypeExpr). Make step-3 pass.; Write failing parser test: parse `|| true` (zero-param lambda) and `|x, y, z| x + y + z` (3-param untyped lambda). Verify edge cases.; Handle zero-param and multi-param lambda edge cases in the parser lowering. Make step-5 pass.; Write failing compiler test: compile a lambda `|x| x * 2` in a let binding context. Verify it produces CompiledExprKind::Lambda with 1 param, no captures, and correct body.; Add ExprKind::Lambda handling to compile_expr_guarded() in reify-compiler/src/lib.rs. Create a nested CompilationScope for the lambda body with lambda params registered. Compile the body expression in that nested scope. Initially, captures = empty vec. Result type is Type::Function. Make step-7 pass.; Write failing compiler test: compile `let factor = 3; |x| x * factor` — verify that the lambda's captures vec contains the ValueCellId for 'factor'.; Implement capture analysis: after compiling the lambda body, walk the CompiledExpr tree collecting all ValueRef(id) references. Filter to only those ids that come from the outer scope (not lambda params). Store as captures in CompiledExprKind::Lambda. Make step-9 pass.; Write failing compiler test: compile lambda with typed params `|x: Real| x + 1.0` — verify param type is recorded as Some(Type::Real) in the compiled output.; Handle typed lambda params in the compiler: resolve type name to Type (using existing type resolution logic). Make step-11 pass.

Write failing eval test: evaluate a lambda expression `|x| x * 2` — verify it produces Value::Lambda with the correct params and captured values.; Add CompiledExprKind::Lambda handling to eval_expr() in reify-expr/src/lib.rs. When evaluating a lambda expression, snapshot the captured ValueCellId values from the current ValueMap into a new ValueMap, then return Value::Lambda { params, body, captures }. Make step-13 pass.; Write failing eval test: evaluate a lambda with captures — `factor=3` in ValueMap, eval `|x| x * factor`. Verify the resulting Value::Lambda captures the factor value from the ValueMap.; Ensure capture evaluation correctly snapshots outer scope values. Make step-15 pass.; Write failing eval test: evaluate a lambda with Undef capture — one captured variable is Undef. Verify the lambda is still created but the capture contains Undef.; Ensure Undef propagation in lambda capture: if a captured value is Undef, it's stored as Undef in the closure (lazy Undef propagation). Make step-17 pass.; Write failing eval test: call/apply a Value::Lambda — create a helper function `apply_lambda(lambda: &Value, args: &[Value]) -> Value` and test `(|x| x * 2)` applied to `[Int(5)]` returns `Int(10)`.; Implement apply_lambda() in reify-expr: given a Value::Lambda and argument values, create a ValueMap with params bound to args plus captures, evaluate the body. Export the function. Make step-19 pass.; Write failing eval test: apply a lambda with captures — `factor=3`, lambda `|x| x * factor`, apply to `[Int(5)]` returns `Int(15)`.; Ensure apply_lambda merges capture ValueMap with param bindings. Make step-21 pass.

All remaining steps (23-28) were already implemented in prior iteration commits. Verified all 16 lambda tests pass across reify-syntax, reify-compiler, and reify-expr. Updated plan.json status to done for steps 23-28. Task 58 (Lambda expressions) is now complete.
Add quantifier_expression rule to tree-sitter grammar.js, regenerate parser.c with `tree-sitter generate`

Completed all 12 steps. Steps 1-8 were already committed from prior iteration. Step-9 (Kleene 3-valued logic tests) was written but uncommitted - committed it. Step-10 confirmed already working from step-6. Step-11 added two end-to-end integration tests (parse+compile+eval) for forall constraint and exists let binding. Step-12 verified all traversal functions (walk, collect_value_refs, collect_body_refs) already handle Quantifier. All tests pass across reify-syntax, reify-compiler, reify-expr.
